query: Playwright vs Cypress vs Selenium vs Puppeteer comparison 2025 features GitHub stars community support
answer:
### Overview
**Playwright** leads in 2025 for speed, cross-browser support, multi-language capabilities, and low maintenance, making it ideal for modern E2E testing suites. Cypress excels in developer experience and JavaScript-only teams but lags in speed and browser support; Selenium offers broad legacy compatibility at the cost of slowness and flakiness; Puppeteer suits Chrome-specific tasks like scraping but lacks scalability.[1][3][6]

### Key Feature Comparison (2025 Data)
The table below synthesizes benchmarks and features from recent comparisons, focusing on setup, performance, support, and maintenance. Ratings draw from execution times, auto-waiting, scalability, and real-world metrics.[1][3]

| Feature                  | Playwright                          | Cypress                             | Selenium                            | Puppeteer                           |
|--------------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| **Setup Time**          | 15-30 min[1]                       | 15-30 min[1]                       | 2-4 hours[1]                       | 10-15 min[1]                       |
| **Performance (Mean Execution)** | 4.513s (fastest)[3]                | 9.378s (slowest)[3]                | 4.590s[3]; slowest overall[1]      | 4.784s; fastest Chrome-only[1][3]  |
| **Cross-Browser**       | Full (Chrome, Firefox, WebKit, mobile emulation)[3] | Limited (Chrome, Firefox, Edge; no WebKit)[3] | All major + IE (via WebDriver)[3]  | Chrome/ Chromium focus; experimental Firefox[3] |
| **Languages**           | JS, Python, .NET, Java[3]          | JS only[3]                         | C#, Python, Ruby, JS, Java, etc.[3]| JS only[3]                         |
| **Auto-Waiting**        | Robust (locator-based)[3]          | Strong[3]                          | None (manual)[3]                   | Basic[3]                           |
| **Scalability**         | Excellent (parallel, CI/CD)[3]     | Excellent[3]                       | Excellent (Grid)[3]                | Poor (no native parallel)[3]       |
| **Mobile**              | Emulation + viewport[1][4]         | Responsive emulation only[4]       | Appium integration for real devices[4] | Chrome emulation[1]                |
| **Maintenance (Hours/Month)** | 12h (30% sprint)[1]                | 10.5h (26%)[1]                     | 16.5h (41%)[1]                     | 11h (28%)[1]                       |
| **Flakiness**           | Low (67% drop vs Cypress)[6]       | Higher retry needs[6]              | High (breaks on UI changes)[1][6]  | Moderate[1]                        |

### GitHub Stars and Community Support
Direct 2025 GitHub stars unavailable in results, but community strength inferred from adoption, docs, and ecosystem:
- **Playwright**: Largest/most active (multi-lang, robust docs, CI/CD plugins); top for scalability and modern teams.[1][3]
- **Cypress**: Strong JS-focused community, excellent DX (samples, launchpad), but JS-only limits breadth.[3]
- **Selenium**: Mature ecosystem (multi-lang, Grid), but declining due to flakiness; best for legacy/enterprise.[1][3]
- **Puppeteer**: Solid Node/Chrome niche (PDF gen, scraping), but smaller scope and docs gaps.[1][3]

### When to Choose Each (2025 Recommendations)
- **Playwright**: Default for new projects needing speed, cross-browser, and multi-lang (e.g., CI/CD-heavy teams).[1][3][6]
- **Cypress**: JS teams prioritizing ease/debugging; avoid for broad browser needs.[1][3]
- **Selenium**: Legacy systems, real mobile via Appium, or non-JS langs where maintenance tolerable.[1][3][4]
- **Puppeteer**: Chrome-only automation/scraping; migrate to Playwright for expansion.[1][3]

Results emphasize Playwright's edge in 2025 amid frequent UI updates, though all require some maintenance unlike AI alternatives mentioned.[1] Benchmarks vary by test; real-world speed depends on suite size.[3]
